articles.title: Pentagon releases initial batch of declassified files detailing UFOs
articles.summary: The Pentagon released a batch of “never‑before‑seen” declassified files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed the agency to do so earlier in the year. The files contain investigations of reported sightings that span decades, including a large FBI file with hundreds of pages of eyewitness testimonies and public reports from 1947 to 1968 and more recent military memos describing a possible small UAP in Iraq in 2022 and multiple glares or lights of unknown origin observed in Syria in 2024. The Pentagon said the documents are now available to the public with no clearance required and will post new materials on a rolling basis every few weeks. A disclaimer on the Pentagon website notes that the descriptive language in the military memos reflects the subjective interpretation of the writer and should not be taken as a conclusive indication of what actually happened. The files also include reports from Apollo 12 astronauts in 1969 who saw flashes of light sailing off the Moon and from Apollo 17 astronauts in 1972 who saw bright particles of light that they theorized might be chunks of ice. Trump’s Truth Social post and NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman’s statement both praised the release as a step toward greater transparency about unidentified anomalous phenomena.

en.title: Pentagon releases initial batch of declassified files detailing UFOs
en.summary: The Pentagon released a batch of “never‑before‑seen” declassified files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed the agency to do so earlier in the year. The files contain investigations of reported sightings that span decades, including a large FBI file with hundreds of pages of eyewitness testimonies and public reports from 1947 to 1968 and more recent military memos describing a possible small UAP in Iraq in 2022 and multiple glares or lights of unknown origin observed in Syria in 2024.

de.title: Das Pentagon veröffentlicht den ersten Stapel freigegebener UFO-Dateien
de.summary: Das Pentagon veröffentlichte eine Reihe „nie zuvor veröffentlichter“ freigegebener UFO-Dateien, nachdem Präsident Donald Trump die Behörde Anfang des Jahres dazu angewiesen hatte. Die Akten enthalten Untersuchungen zu gemeldeten Sichtungen, die sich über Jahrzehnte erstrecken, darunter eine große FBI-Akte mit Hunderten von Seiten an Augenzeugenaussagen und öffentlichen Berichten von 1947 bis 1968 sowie neuere militärische Memos, die eine mögliche kleine UAP im Irak im Jahr 2022 und mehrere in Syrien im Jahr 2024 beobachtete Blendungen oder Lichter unbekannter Herkunft beschreiben.

articles.title: Multiple Pastors Claim U.S. Military Intelligence Officers Secretly Briefed Them To Prepare Their Congregations For UAP Disclosure
articles.summary: Several religious leaders, including Pastor Perry Stone and evangelist Tony Merkel, claim they were privately briefed by U.S. military intelligence officers to prepare their congregations for an upcoming UAP disclosure. Stone, a Tennessee‑based Pentecostal evangelist, said in a video that a trusted associate informed him that a group of pastors was invited to hear directly from government‑connected individuals about an imminent disclosure event involving unidentified anomalous phenomena. Stone described the briefing as covering craft not allegedly part of our planet, non‑human beings, and a coordinated propaganda plan already in motion. Merkel says he has been in contact with officials for over a year, describing them as Christians in intelligence operations assigned to work within the UAP disclosure community, and that they approached him with a mission to prepare the body of Christ for what is coming. The state where the briefing took place has not been disclosed, and no government memo, official invitation, transcript, or attendee list has surfaced. The White House has not denied the claims, and Washington has remained silent, a silence the article notes is significant.

de.title: Mehrere Pastoren behaupten, US-Militärgeheimdienstoffiziere hätten sie heimlich informiert, um ihre Gemeinden auf die UAP-Offenlegung vorzubereiten
de.summary: S. Offiziere des Militärgeheimdienstes, um ihre Gemeinden auf eine bevorstehende UAP-Enthüllung vorzubereiten. Stone, ein in Tennessee ansässiger Pfingstevangelist, sagte in einem Video, ein vertrauenswürdiger Mitarbeiter habe ihn darüber informiert, dass eine Gruppe von Pastoren eingeladen worden sei, direkt von Personen mit Regierungskontakten über eine bevorstehende Offenlegung unbekannter anomaler Phänomene zu hören.

articles.title: ‘SLEEPING DOG’ Trailer Dives Into Jeremy Corbell’s Fight For UAP Disclosure
articles.summary: The trailer for “SLEEPING DOG” offers a first look at a documentary that follows investigative journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell and his controversial rise in the UAP disclosure movement. Directed by Michael Lazovsky, the film has been described as a real‑life “Disclosure Day” and charts Corbell’s transition from mixed martial artist to a prominent figure pushing for transparency around unidentified aerial phenomena. It will open in select theaters on May 8, 2026, before becoming available on digital platforms on May 12. The documentary explores Corbell’s long partnership with investigative reporter George Knapp and their attempts to bring military‑filmed UAP footage and whistleblower testimony to the public eye. It promises unprecedented access and never‑before‑seen footage from Corbell’s earliest investigations, featuring figures such as astronaut Edgar Mitchell, John Lear, Bob Lazar, David Grusch, David Fravor, John B. Alexander and others involved in the UAP transparency movement. The film is produced by Jeremy Corbell, Jim Martin and Michael Lazovsky and aims to provide an intimate, high‑stakes look at one of the most polarizing subjects of the modern era.

en.summary: The trailer for “SLEEPING DOG” offers a first look at a documentary that follows investigative journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell and his controversial rise in the UAP disclosure movement. Directed by Michael Lazovsky, the film has been described as a real‑life “Disclosure Day” and charts Corbell’s transition from mixed martial artist to a prominent figure pushing for transparency around unidentified aerial phenomena. It will open in select theaters on May 8, 2026, before becoming available on digital platforms on May 12.

de.title: Der Trailer zu „SLEEPING DOG“ befasst sich mit Jeremy Corbells Kampf für die Offenlegung der UAP
de.summary: Der Trailer zu „SLEEPING DOG“ bietet einen ersten Blick auf einen Dokumentarfilm, der den investigativen Journalisten und Filmemacher Jeremy Corbell und seinen umstrittenen Aufstieg in der UAP-Offenlegungsbewegung begleitet. Der von Michael Lazovsky inszenierte Film wurde als „Disclosure Day“ im wirklichen Leben beschrieben und zeigt Corbells Übergang vom Mixed Martial Artist zu einer prominenten Figur, die sich für Transparenz bei unbekannten Luftphänomenen einsetzt. Der Film startet am 8. Mai 2026 in ausgewählten Kinos, bevor er am 12. Mai auf digitalen Plattformen verfügbar ist.

articles.title: UAP Whistleblower David Grusch on “Non-human” Biologics & Craft
articles.summary: David Grusch served 14 years in the Air Force, reaching the rank of Major and completing a combat tour in Afghanistan. He held senior intelligence positions at the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency and the National Reconnaissance Office. Grusch represented the NRO on the Unidentified Aerial Phenomena Task Force from 2019 to 2021. In 2022 he filed a whistleblower complaint that was described as credible and urgent. In 2023 he testified before Congress. He claimed that elements of the U.S. government had thwarted Congressional oversight, withheld information from proper Executive Branch officials, and illegally concealed programs related to the recovery and reverse engineering of craft of unknown, non‑human origin.

Er hatte leitende Geheimdienstpositionen bei der National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency und dem National Reconnaissance Office inne. Von 2019 bis 2021 vertrat Grusch die NRO in der Unidentified Aerial Phenomena Task Force.

articles.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna Frames UFO Disclosure Through The Book of Enoch As A Window Into A Reality Beyond Human Perception
articles.summary: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna of Florida publicly suggested that the ongoing discussion about unidentified aerial phenomena may be closely connected to themes found in the Book of Enoch. She frames this link through the interdimensional hypothesis and Carl Sagan’s Flatland teaching model, arguing that both the modern UAP conversation and the ancient text describe interactions between different layers of existence. The UAP conversation, acknowledged by NASA and the Department of Defense, includes documented observations of sudden acceleration, seamless trans‑medium travel, and behavior that does not align with known propulsion systems or aerodynamics. Luna’s remarks emphasize that the Book of Enoch is not presented as scientific evidence but as an early attempt to describe encounters that lacked a clear vocabulary, mirroring the structural parallels of the interdimensional hypothesis. She does not claim that UAPs are definitively linked to the Watchers of the Book of Enoch, noting that no verified evidence currently supports such a connection. The article warns that introducing religious or historical texts into a policy‑driven discussion carries risks of misinterpretation and speculation, while acknowledging that the disclosure process is revealing complexity rather than simple answers.

de.title: Die Abgeordnete Anna Paulina Luna stellt die UFO-Enthüllung anhand des Buches Henoch als Fenster in eine Realität dar, die jenseits der menschlichen Wahrnehmung liegt

Sie formuliert diesen Zusammenhang anhand der interdimensionalen Hypothese und des Flatland-Lehrmodells von Carl Sagan und argumentiert, dass sowohl die moderne UAP-Konversation als auch der antike Text Interaktionen zwischen verschiedenen Ebenen der Existenz beschreiben.

articles.title: The Rendlesham Forest mystery: ‘It’s the perfect storm of a UFO case’
articles.summary: Nick Pope, who worked for the UK Ministry of Defence from 1985 to 2006 and headed the UFO desk from 1991 to 1994, said in a 1996 interview that after reviewing evidence he believed that 5–10 % of UFO sightings could not be explained by conventional means and might be extraterrestrial craft. Pope’s research led him to focus on the Rendlesham Forest incident of 24–26 December 1980, when two American airmen reported strange blinking red, blue and white lights near RAF Bentwaters, a US base that housed nuclear missiles. The case is described by Pope as a “perfect storm” because it involved multiple military witnesses, radar confirmation, physical evidence such as ground indentations and scorch marks, and declassified documents now available at the National Archives and the MoD website. Pope co‑authored the 2014 book Encounter in Rendlesham Forest with eyewitnesses Jim Penniston and John Burroughs, who described how they investigated the lights, found a triangular craft with no landing gear, and observed a bright, static‑like burst of light that left them stunned. Penniston reported that the craft was about six and a half feet tall, had a dorsal fin and engravings resembling Egyptian hieroglyphs, and that its surface felt warm and smooth, while Burroughs was frozen in a beam of white/blue light. The incident remains unexplained, with the witnesses noting that the craft did not move when they attempted to push it and that the surrounding area showed unusual indentations, suggesting technology beyond current human capability.

en.title: The Rendlesham Forest mystery: ‘It’s the perfect storm of a UFO case’
en.summary: Nick Pope, who worked for the UK Ministry of Defence from 1985 to 2006 and headed the UFO desk from 1991 to 1994, said in a 1996 interview that after reviewing evidence he believed that 5–10 % of UFO sightings could not be explained by conventional means and might be extraterrestrial craft. Pope’s research led him to focus on the Rendlesham Forest incident of 24–26 December 1980, when two American airmen reported strange blinking red, blue and white lights near RAF Bentwaters, a US base that housed nuclear missiles.

de.title: Das Rätsel um den Rendlesham Forest: „Es ist der perfekte Sturm eines UFO-Falls“
de.summary: Nick Pope, der von 1985 bis 2006 für das britische Verteidigungsministerium arbeitete und von 1991 bis 1994 die UFO-Abteilung leitete, sagte 1996 in einem Interview, dass er nach Prüfung der Beweise davon ausgegangen sei, dass 5–10 % der UFO-Sichtungen nicht mit konventionellen Mitteln erklärt werden könnten und dass es sich möglicherweise um außerirdische Schiffe handele. Popes Nachforschungen führten dazu, dass er sich auf den Vorfall im Rendlesham Forest vom 24. bis 26. Dezember 1980 konzentrierte, als zwei amerikanische Flieger seltsam blinkende rote, blaue und weiße Lichter in der Nähe von RAF Bentwaters meldeten, einem US-Stützpunkt, auf dem Atomraketen stationiert waren.

The article is part of a cluster of MSN sources covering UFO-related topics. No additional details are available in the description.

de.title: UFO-Whistleblower geben Warnung heraus, nachdem ein Luftwaffenoffizier tot aufgefunden wurde, bevor er aussagen konnte
de.summary: Laut einem Bericht von MSN vom 29. April 2026 gaben UFO-Whistleblower eine Warnung heraus, nachdem ein Luftwaffenoffizier tot aufgefunden wurde, bevor er aussagen konnte. In dem Bericht heißt es, dass der Tod des Beamten vor seiner geplanten Aussage eingetreten sei. Die Warnung des Whistleblowers steht im Zusammenhang mit der geplanten Aussage des Beamten zu UFO.

summaries.batch-declassified-detailing-files-initi: The Pentagon released a batch of “never‑before‑seen” declassified files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed the agency to do so earlier in the year. The files contain investigations of reported sightings that span decades, including a large FBI file with hundreds of pages of eyewitness testimonies and public reports from 1947 to 1968 and more recent military memos describing a possible small UAP in Iraq in 2022 and multiple glares or lights of unknown origin observed in Syria in 2024. The Pentagon said the documents are now available to the public with no clearance required and will post new materials on a rolling basis every few weeks. A disclaimer on the Pentagon website notes that the descriptive language in the military memos reflects the subjective interpretation of the writer and should not be taken as a conclusive indication of what actually happened. The files also include reports from Apollo 12 astronauts in 1969 who saw flashes of light sailing off the Moon and from Apollo 17 astronauts in 1972 who saw bright particles of light that they theorized might be chunks of ice. Trump’s Truth Social post and NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman’s statement both praised the release as a step toward greater transparency about unidentified anomalous phenomena.
summaries.briefed-claim-congregations-disclosure-i: Several religious leaders, including Pastor Perry Stone and evangelist Tony Merkel, claim they were privately briefed by U.S. military intelligence officers to prepare their congregations for an upcoming UAP disclosure. Stone, a Tennessee‑based Pentecostal evangelist, said in a video that a trusted associate informed him that a group of pastors was invited to hear directly from government‑connected individuals about an imminent disclosure event involving unidentified anomalous phenomena. Stone described the briefing as covering craft not allegedly part of our planet, non‑human beings, and a coordinated propaganda plan already in motion. Merkel says he has been in contact with officials for over a year, describing them as Christians in intelligence operations assigned to work within the UAP disclosure community, and that they approached him with a mission to prepare the body of Christ for what is coming. The state where the briefing took place has not been disclosed, and no government memo, official invitation, transcript, or attendee list has surfaced. The White House has not denied the claims, and Washington has remained silent, a silence the article notes is significant.
summaries.corbell-disclosure-dives-dog-fight-jerem: The trailer for “SLEEPING DOG” offers a first look at a documentary that follows investigative journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell and his controversial rise in the UAP disclosure movement. Directed by Michael Lazovsky, the film has been described as a real‑life “Disclosure Day” and charts Corbell’s transition from mixed martial artist to a prominent figure pushing for transparency around unidentified aerial phenomena. It will open in select theaters on May 8, 2026, before becoming available on digital platforms on May 12. The documentary explores Corbell’s long partnership with investigative reporter George Knapp and their attempts to bring military‑filmed UAP footage and whistleblower testimony to the public eye. It promises unprecedented access and never‑before‑seen footage from Corbell’s earliest investigations, featuring figures such as astronaut Edgar Mitchell, John Lear, Bob Lazar, David Grusch, David Fravor, John B. Alexander and others involved in the UAP transparency movement. The film is produced by Jeremy Corbell, Jim Martin and Michael Lazovsky and aims to provide an intimate, high‑stakes look at one of the most polarizing subjects of the modern era.
summaries.biologics-craft-david-grusch-human-non-w: David Grusch served 14 years in the Air Force, reaching the rank of Major and completing a combat tour in Afghanistan. He held senior intelligence positions at the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency and the National Reconnaissance Office. Grusch represented the NRO on the Unidentified Aerial Phenomena Task Force from 2019 to 2021. In 2022 he filed a whistleblower complaint that was described as credible and urgent. In 2023 he testified before Congress. He claimed that elements of the U.S. government had thwarted Congressional oversight, withheld information from proper Executive Branch officials, and illegally concealed programs related to the recovery and reverse engineering of craft of unknown, non‑human origin.
summaries.anna-beyond-book-disclosure-enoch-frames: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna of Florida publicly suggested that the ongoing discussion about unidentified aerial phenomena may be closely connected to themes found in the Book of Enoch. She frames this link through the interdimensional hypothesis and Carl Sagan’s Flatland teaching model, arguing that both the modern UAP conversation and the ancient text describe interactions between different layers of existence. The UAP conversation, acknowledged by NASA and the Department of Defense, includes documented observations of sudden acceleration, seamless trans‑medium travel, and behavior that does not align with known propulsion systems or aerodynamics. Luna’s remarks emphasize that the Book of Enoch is not presented as scientific evidence but as an early attempt to describe encounters that lacked a clear vocabulary, mirroring the structural parallels of the interdimensional hypothesis. She does not claim that UAPs are definitively linked to the Watchers of the Book of Enoch, noting that no verified evidence currently supports such a connection. The article warns that introducing religious or historical texts into a policy‑driven discussion carries risks of misinterpretation and speculation, while acknowledging that the disclosure process is revealing complexity rather than simple answers.
summaries.accused-alien-backing-bias-claims-conspi: International Business Times UK lists a UAP-related report dated 2026-04-29 under the headline "UFO War Ignites As Tim Gallaudet Accused Of Conspiracy Bias And Backing Fringe Alien Tech Claims". The headline is treated as the source claim, and UAP News does not add details that are not present in the available feed metadata. The topic is connected with ALIEN in the feed metadata.
summaries.case-forest-mystery-perfect-rendlesham-s: Nick Pope, who worked for the UK Ministry of Defence from 1985 to 2006 and headed the UFO desk from 1991 to 1994, said in a 1996 interview that after reviewing evidence he believed that 5–10 % of UFO sightings could not be explained by conventional means and might be extraterrestrial craft. Pope’s research led him to focus on the Rendlesham Forest incident of 24–26 December 1980, when two American airmen reported strange blinking red, blue and white lights near RAF Bentwaters, a US base that housed nuclear missiles. The case is described by Pope as a “perfect storm” because it involved multiple military witnesses, radar confirmation, physical evidence such as ground indentations and scorch marks, and declassified documents now available at the National Archives and the MoD website. Pope co‑authored the 2014 book Encounter in Rendlesham Forest with eyewitnesses Jim Penniston and John Burroughs, who described how they investigated the lights, found a triangular craft with no landing gear, and observed a bright, static‑like burst of light that left them stunned. Penniston reported that the craft was about six and a half feet tall, had a dorsal fin and engravings resembling Egyptian hieroglyphs, and that its surface felt warm and smooth, while Burroughs was frozen in a beam of white/blue light. The incident remains unexplained, with the witnesses noting that the craft did not move when they attempted to push it and that the surrounding area showed unusual indentations, suggesting technology beyond current human capability.
summaries.common-insider-interesting-missing-scien: A NewsNation article dated April 27, 2026 reports that a UFO insider claims missing scientists share interesting common ties. The claim is presented in the headline, which states that the missing scientists have “interesting common ties.” No description of the article is available in the feed metadata. The article is part of a cluster of NewsNation sources. No additional context or details are provided in the brief.
summaries.claims-david-death-growing-missing-narra: The MSN article dated May 5, 2026 reports that David Wilcock has died. It describes his death as the latest in a growing narrative of missing U.S. scientists tied to UFO claims. The piece frames Wilcock’s death within this broader context of alleged disappearances linked to extraterrestrial investigations. The article is sourced from MSN.
summaries.actually-files-interesting-release-they: On May 4, 2026, OregonLive.com published an article titled “Release the UFO files! (If they’re actually interesting).” The headline calls for the release of UFO files, implying that the files might be of interest. The article is part of a broader discussion about unidentified aerial phenomena. No additional details are available in the metadata. The piece is sourced solely from OregonLive.com.

The report states that the officer’s death occurred prior to his scheduled testimony. The whistleblowers’ warning is linked to the officer’s planned testimony on UFO. The article is part of a cluster of MSN sources covering UFO-related topics. No additional details are available in the description.

en.title: Pentagon releases initial batch of declassified files detailing UFOs
en.summary: The Pentagon released a batch of “never‑before‑seen” declassified files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed the agency to do so earlier in the year. The files contain investigations of reported sightings that span decades, including a large FBI file with hundreds of pages of eyewitness testimonies and public reports from 1947 to 1968 and more recent military memos describing a possible small UAP in Iraq in 2022 and multiple glares or lights of unknown origin observed in Syria in 2024.

de.title: Das Pentagon veröffentlicht den ersten Stapel freigegebener UFO-Dateien
de.summary: Das Pentagon veröffentlichte eine Reihe „nie zuvor veröffentlichter“ freigegebener UFO-Dateien, nachdem Präsident Donald Trump die Behörde Anfang des Jahres dazu angewiesen hatte. Die Akten enthalten Untersuchungen zu gemeldeten Sichtungen, die sich über Jahrzehnte erstrecken, darunter eine große FBI-Akte mit Hunderten von Seiten an Augenzeugenaussagen und öffentlichen Berichten von 1947 bis 1968 sowie neuere militärische Memos, die eine mögliche kleine UAP im Irak im Jahr 2022 und mehrere in Syrien im Jahr 2024 beobachtete Blendungen oder Lichter unbekannter Herkunft beschreiben.

en.title: Multiple Pastors Claim U.S. Military Intelligence Officers Secretly Briefed Them To Prepare Their Congregations For UAP Disclosure
en.summary: S. military intelligence officers to prepare their congregations for an upcoming UAP disclosure. Stone, a Tennessee‑based Pentecostal evangelist, said in a video that a trusted associate informed him that a group of pastors was invited to hear directly from government‑connected individuals about an imminent disclosure event involving unidentified anomalous phenomena.

de.title: Mehrere Pastoren behaupten, US-Militärgeheimdienstoffiziere hätten sie heimlich informiert, um ihre Gemeinden auf die UAP-Offenlegung vorzubereiten
de.summary: S. Offiziere des Militärgeheimdienstes, um ihre Gemeinden auf eine bevorstehende UAP-Enthüllung vorzubereiten. Stone, ein in Tennessee ansässiger Pfingstevangelist, sagte in einem Video, ein vertrauenswürdiger Mitarbeiter habe ihn darüber informiert, dass eine Gruppe von Pastoren eingeladen worden sei, direkt von Personen mit Regierungskontakten über eine bevorstehende Offenlegung unbekannter anomaler Phänomene zu hören.

en.title: ‘SLEEPING DOG’ Trailer Dives Into Jeremy Corbell’s Fight For UAP Disclosure
en.summary: The trailer for “SLEEPING DOG” offers a first look at a documentary that follows investigative journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell and his controversial rise in the UAP disclosure movement. Directed by Michael Lazovsky, the film has been described as a real‑life “Disclosure Day” and charts Corbell’s transition from mixed martial artist to a prominent figure pushing for transparency around unidentified aerial phenomena. It will open in select theaters on May 8, 2026, before becoming available on digital platforms on May 12.

de.title: Der Trailer zu „SLEEPING DOG“ befasst sich mit Jeremy Corbells Kampf für die Offenlegung der UAP
de.summary: Der Trailer zu „SLEEPING DOG“ bietet einen ersten Blick auf einen Dokumentarfilm, der den investigativen Journalisten und Filmemacher Jeremy Corbell und seinen umstrittenen Aufstieg in der UAP-Offenlegungsbewegung begleitet. Der von Michael Lazovsky inszenierte Film wurde als „Disclosure Day“ im wirklichen Leben beschrieben und zeigt Corbells Übergang vom Mixed Martial Artist zu einer prominenten Figur, die sich für Transparenz bei unbekannten Luftphänomenen einsetzt. Der Film startet am 8. Mai 2026 in ausgewählten Kinos, bevor er am 12. Mai auf digitalen Plattformen verfügbar ist.

en.title: UAP Whistleblower David Grusch on “Non-human” Biologics & Craft
en.summary: David Grusch served 14 years in the Air Force, reaching the rank of Major and completing a combat tour in Afghanistan. He held senior intelligence positions at the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency and the National Reconnaissance Office. Grusch represented the NRO on the Unidentified Aerial Phenomena Task Force from 2019 to 2021.

de.title: UAP-Whistleblower David Grusch über „nichtmenschliche“ Biologika und Handwerk
de.summary: David Grusch diente 14 Jahre lang in der Luftwaffe, erreichte den Rang eines Majors und absolvierte einen Kampfeinsatz in Afghanistan. Er hatte leitende Geheimdienstpositionen bei der National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency und dem National Reconnaissance Office inne. Von 2019 bis 2021 vertrat Grusch die NRO in der Unidentified Aerial Phenomena Task Force.

en.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna Frames UFO Disclosure Through The Book of Enoch As A Window Into A Reality Beyond Human Perception
en.summary: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna of Florida publicly suggested that the ongoing discussion about unidentified aerial phenomena may be closely connected to themes found in the Book of Enoch. She frames this link through the interdimensional hypothesis and Carl Sagan’s Flatland teaching model, arguing that both the modern UAP conversation and the ancient text describe interactions between different layers of existence.

de.title: Die Abgeordnete Anna Paulina Luna stellt die UFO-Enthüllung anhand des Buches Henoch als Fenster in eine Realität dar, die jenseits der menschlichen Wahrnehmung liegt
de.summary: Die Abgeordnete Anna Paulina Luna aus Florida wies öffentlich darauf hin, dass die laufende Diskussion über nicht identifizierte Luftphänomene eng mit Themen im Buch Henoch verbunden sein könnte. Sie formuliert diesen Zusammenhang anhand der interdimensionalen Hypothese und des Flatland-Lehrmodells von Carl Sagan und argumentiert, dass sowohl die moderne UAP-Konversation als auch der antike Text Interaktionen zwischen verschiedenen Ebenen der Existenz beschreiben.

de.summary: Die International Business Times UK listet einen UAP-bezogenen Bericht vom 29.04.2026 mit der Überschrift „UFO-Krieg entfacht, da Tim Gallaudet der Verschwörungsvoreingenommenheit und der Unterstützung von Randbehauptungen von Alien-Tech beschuldigt“ wird. en.title: The Rendlesham Forest mystery: ‘It’s the perfect storm of a UFO case’
en.summary: Nick Pope, who worked for the UK Ministry of Defence from 1985 to 2006 and headed the UFO desk from 1991 to 1994, said in a 1996 interview that after reviewing evidence he believed that 5–10 % of UFO sightings could not be explained by conventional means and might be extraterrestrial craft. Pope’s research led him to focus on the Rendlesham Forest incident of 24–26 December 1980, when two American airmen reported strange blinking red, blue and white lights near RAF Bentwaters, a US base that housed nuclear missiles.

de.title: Das Rätsel um den Rendlesham Forest: „Es ist der perfekte Sturm eines UFO-Falls“
de.summary: Nick Pope, der von 1985 bis 2006 für das britische Verteidigungsministerium arbeitete und von 1991 bis 1994 die UFO-Abteilung leitete, sagte 1996 in einem Interview, dass er nach Prüfung der Beweise davon ausgegangen sei, dass 5–10 % der UFO-Sichtungen nicht mit konventionellen Mitteln erklärt werden könnten und dass es sich möglicherweise um außerirdische Schiffe handele. Popes Nachforschungen führten dazu, dass er sich auf den Vorfall im Rendlesham Forest vom 24. bis 26. Dezember 1980 konzentrierte, als zwei amerikanische Flieger seltsam blinkende rote, blaue und weiße Lichter in der Nähe von RAF Bentwaters meldeten, einem US-Stützpunkt, auf dem Atomraketen stationiert waren.
